data.title: Fmr. Minneapolis Fed president: It is ‘difficult to build rationale’ for lowering rates
data.description:
Former Minneapolis Fed President Gary Stern believes there’s “no compelling rationale to lower rates right away.”

Stern’s cautious approach aligns with recent statements from Boston Fed President Collins, suggesting a preference for slowing down the pace of monetary policy adjustments despite previous rate cuts.

During a CNBC interview, the veteran central banker emphasized the current economic conditions don’t warrant immediate action, citing stubborn inflation “in the 2.5-3% plus range” as a key concern.

“Given where the economy is, the way it’s performing, and the stubbornness of inflation… it seems to me it’s difficult to build a rationale, to build a case for lowering rates at this point,” he said.

Stern highlighted that employment gains continue but remain modest, while consumer spending has generally held up well.

He expressed skepticism about dramatic changes in economic conditions in the near term, noting: “I’d be surprised if in the next month or two or three, we got a very different picture from the data.”

The former Fed official cautioned against overreacting to individual data points, emphasizing the inherently “noisy” nature of economic indicators.

“It’s always a good idea to not only do the analysis, but to smooth out the readings you get month after month and see if the trajectory of the economy is changing,” he said.

Regarding the labor market, Stern stressed the importance of understanding its dynamic nature rather than focusing on isolated corporate announcements.

“We tend to have a very dynamic labor market month after month after month… if the net increase in employment in a month is, say, 50,000, underpinning that is probably a million people who either quit their jobs or lost their jobs and a million plus 50,000 who were hired,” he explained, warning against emphasizing “any one report coming out of whatever company you may be following.”

Odds of a December 25-basis-point interest rate cut have fallen to 47.6%, according to the CME FedWatch tool, while odds of no change are at 52.4%.

More on U.S. economy:

  • Fed policy needs to stay 'somewhat restrictive', Cleveland Fed's Hammack says
  • Mortgage rates essentially flat
  • October jobs report won't have unemployment rate, Hassett says
  • Jobs, CPI reports for October may never be released - White House

data.title: Pure Cycle outlines 2026 recurring revenue growth and single-family rental acceleration as housing headwinds persist
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Pure Cycle Corporation (PCYO) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Mark Harding, President and CEO, emphasized, "continued profitability where you've got 25 straight quarters of profitability and this quarter and this year is no different." He highlighted "continued growth in each of the revenue segments, especially in the recurring revenue segments," with stable earnings across water, wastewater, land development, and rental income. Harding noted, "Q4 is typically our strongest quarter," but revenue was "slightly down, mostly due to the housing headwinds and pushing some of that revenue recognition from our percent completion into Q1 2026."
  • Harding reported that "net income and earnings per share... actually exceeded our expectations" due to oil and gas royalty income coming in stronger than projected, citing "the completion of an additional 6 or 7 wells into the largest portion of our royalty estate" as a key driver.
  • The water utility segment was described as having "a little over 1,600 commercial connection points... out of a total of 60,000 potential," with recurring revenues paced on this 60,000 connection guidance. Tap fees were noted at "right around that $40,000 mark."
  • The land development segment delivered "228 lots of Phase 2C that we had forecasted for 2025," with deferred revenue of about $800,000 spilling into Q1 2026 due to regulatory delays. Harding stated that "overall, sales in Land Development were off then from our expectations, and that was largely attributable to some of the headwinds that we're seeing in housing."
  • The single-family rental segment experienced delays from regulatory updates but has "about 40 homes under contract now that are delivering from several of our homebuilders," with expectations for a "substantial increase in the number of single-family rentals in 2026 and into 2027."
  • Harding mentioned a "continued strong balance sheet," with investment focus on asset growth, liquidity, and share repurchases.
  • CFO Marc Spezialy did not provide a separate prepared statement in the transcript.

Outlook

  • Harding projected that in 2026, recurring revenue is expected to continue growing, "not only from our water customers, but also some of our single-family rental," and forecasted a build-out target of "as many as 250 to 300 units" for single-family rentals. He outlined a short-term objective to reach "up to about 100 units" in the rental segment and described the land development segment as moving toward "about 30%" completion of Sky Ranch in the next period.
  • Management signaled a "range of earnings per share" for 2026, with variance tied to the sensitivity of lot deliveries and industrial water sales, but did not disclose specific guidance figures in the transcript.

Financial Results

  • Harding stated, "revenue for Q4, again, it was our highest quarter, slightly down, mostly due to the housing headwinds and pushing some of that revenue recognition from our percent completion into Q1 2026."
  • Despite revenue and gross profit being "slightly off from what we saw in 2024," net income and EPS "exceeded our expectations," attributed to stronger oil and gas royalty income.
  • The company reported "forecasted net income was right around $12.5 million, slightly lower revenue from our land development segment," with higher costs of revenue due to tariffs and inflation, but offset by lower G&A expenses and higher oil and gas royalties.
  • On the water segment, annual revenues are described as "about $1,600 per connection per year," with the recurring customer base growing at a "22% CAGR."
  • The land development segment had deferred revenues and regulatory delays impacting timing, with some revenue shifting into the next fiscal year.

Q&A

  • Greg Vennett, a shareholder, asked, "Has housing sales in your areas slowed down due to affordability or... are the builders still building?" Harding responded that "Denver is probably on the high side of unaffordability," but Pure Cycle's entry-level segment remains resilient, stating "we are in that affordable market segment... probably in that 4% of homes that are delivered on an annual basis are in that affordable price segment."
  • An unnamed analyst inquired about land acquisitions and commercial development. Harding replied, "we really do have our nets out, and we're saving a bit of liquidity just for that," adding that conversations around acquisitions are strengthening, but timing is critical. On commercial, he noted "we are seeing interest from a lot of those" and that growth projected in 2028 is "mostly a factor of being able to open up Phase 3 with the interchange."
  • The analyst added, "the near-term weakness that you're seeing from some of the homebuilders may be a long-term benefit for you guys in that the land may not be as expensive." Harding acknowledged, "we're very disciplined in what we pay" and that some sellers may be motivated by timing rather than price.
  • Vennett also asked about competition in water provision and commercial development. Harding stated, "we're the best provider, but we're not the only," citing City of Aurora as main competition and describing Pure Cycle's advantages in location and cost structure.
  • Matt Reiner, Aranda, asked about lower 2026 earnings forecast despite higher revenues. Harding explained, "2025 had a high profitability because of kind of the oil and gas, and that's almost 100% margin...we're not likely to see the same earnings per share bump that we saw in 2025 because of that profitability of the oil and gas royalties."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed slightly negative sentiment, focusing on housing headwinds, acquisition timing, and competitive advantages. Questions pressed for detail on margin differences, cost structure, and the impact of market softness.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing flexibility and recurring growth, stating "the resiliency of our builders and our project is strengthened by the fact that we are in that affordable market segment" and "we're very disciplined in what we pay."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed a more cautious tone, with increased attention to headwinds and regulatory delays, but management continued to stress business model flexibility and multiple revenue streams.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw management address slightly lower revenue and gross profit due to housing headwinds and regulatory delays, while net income and EPS were boosted by oil and gas royalty outperformance.
  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize recurring revenue growth and single-family rental acceleration, with a cautious approach to land development and acquisitions.
  • Analyst questions this quarter probed deeper into acquisition opportunities, commercial development timing, and margin sustainability, compared to the previous quarter's focus on operational progress and water asset development.
  • Key metric changes included shifting revenue recognition into the next fiscal year and a notable increase in single-family rental targets.
  • Management's confidence remained steady, but the tone reflected greater market caution and focus on operational agility.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted housing market headwinds and regulatory delays as key challenges, with some land development revenue deferred into the next year.
  • Cost inflation and tariffs were noted as contributing to higher costs of revenue, though mitigated by controlling SG&A expenses and diversifying revenue streams.
  • Analysts expressed concern about acquisition timing, competitive landscape in water provision, and the sustainability of high-margin oil and gas royalties.

Final Takeaway

Pure Cycle Corporation reported a solid quarter of profitability, with earnings per share slightly exceeding expectations due to strong oil and gas royalties, even as revenue and gross profit were impacted by housing market headwinds and regulatory delays. Management signaled continued growth in recurring revenue segments, an accelerated expansion of single-family rentals, and a disciplined approach to land and commercial development acquisitions. The outlook emphasizes operational flexibility and capital discipline as the company maneuvers through housing affordability challenges and positions itself for long-term asset growth and monetization across its core business segments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Enterprise Products Partners on track to extend gains for the seventh straight trading day
data.description:
Enterprise Products Partners (EPD) is on track to close in green for the seventh consecutive trading session, with the stock trading 1.50% higher at $32.05 during Thursday afternoon.

The Houston, Texas-based midstream energy services provider has added 4.40% over the course of the last six trading days.

A notable gain was logged on Wednesday, November 5. The stock closed the day 1.49% higher at $30.67. Enterprise Products Partners filed its quarterly report with the Securities and Exchange Commission on the day.

The company reported mixed financial results on October 30, with the Q3 GAAP EPS missing the Wall Street analysts' estimates and revenue surpassing consensus.

Notably, Enterprise Products reported an increase in its Q3 NGL, crude, refined products, and petrochemical transportation volumes.

"While EPD is suffering from some project delays and is currently in a zone where leverage is rising a bit due to having to lean into spending on growth investments, the future looks very bright for the company," said Seeking Alpha analyst Samuel Smith.

"It is rapidly approaching its cash flow inflection point and appears poised to return a considerable amount of cash flow to unit holders while also seeing its leverage ratio drop significantly," added Smith.

On average, SA authors and the Wall Street analysts grade the stock as Buy.

Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha Quant Rating system gives EPD a Hold rating, with a score of 3.04 on a scale of 5. The stock is rated C+ for Valuation; C- for Growth, Momentum, and Revisions; and A for Profitability.

data.title: Bitcoin drops below $100K as market selloff intensifies
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) extended its recent decline on Thursday, falling in tandem with broader market weakness and slipping back below the closely watched $100,000 level.

The world’s largest cryptocurrency was last seen trading near $98,350, down about 3.1% on the day. A close below $100,000 would mark bitcoin’s first finish under that threshold since May 7, which would represent a six-month closing low. 

From a technical standpoint, bitcoin’s relative strength index currently stands at 40.14 — not yet in oversold territory, but approaching that range. This marks the weakest RSI reading since mid-March, signaling that selling pressure has intensified. Momentum indicators also point to ongoing softness, as bitcoin now trades below its 20-, 50-, 100-, and 200-day moving averages, suggesting the trend has turned broadly negative across multiple time frames. 

Despite the pullback, investor interest in crypto-related assets remains elevated, particularly through exchange-traded funds that provide exposure to bitcoin’s price movements without direct ownership. 

Notable bitcoin-linked ETFs include the iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF (ARKB), Grayscale Bitcoin Trust (GBTC), Valkyrie Bitcoin Fund (BRRR), Invesco Galaxy Bitcoin ETF (BTCO), VanEck Bitcoin Trust (HODL), WisdomTree Bitcoin Fund (BTCW), Fidelity Wise Origin Bitcoin ETF (FBTC), Bitwise Bitcoin ETF (BITB), and Franklin Bitcoin ETF (EZBC).

data.title: Bitcoin slides below $100K, dragging down Coinbase, Robinhood, miners, others
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) broke below $100K on Thursday afternoon as investors pulled back from risk, pulling down crypto-related stocks. Equity markets also retreated as the process of restarting the U.S. government may be a bumpy one. Economists fear that some of the economic data usually produced by the federal government may never be recovered, as data collection halted during the 43-day shutdown.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) fell 3.1% to ~$98.4K at about 1:44 PM ET. Other cryptocurrencies followed suit.

"We would not be surprised to see some market chop over the coming weeks as the government gears and economic data presses get turning again," said Carol Schleif, chief market strategist at BMO Private Wealth.

In addition, the odds of the Federal Reserve cutting rates further have declined. Lower interest rates encourage spending and economic growth. Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said the Fed's policy should remain "somewhat restrictive," but right now she considers it to be "barely restrictive." 

Among other cryptocurrencies, ethereum (ETH-USD) slid 6.7% to ~$3.21K, solana (SOL-USD) fell 5.0%, cardano (ADA-USD) decreased 5.0%, and Doge coin (DOGE-USD) dropped 3.6%.

The crypto downdraft dragged miners and other crypto-related stocks with it. MARA Holdings (MARA) tumbled 10%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) slid 11%, Hut 8 (HUT) lost 13%, and Hive Digital (HIVE) fell 10%.

Coinbase Global (COIN), the crypto exchange, retreated 6.6%, Bullish (BLSH) declined 8.7%, Robinhood Markets (HOOD) fell 10%, and Strategy (MSTR) slid 6.2%.

data.title: NEM set to snap six straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Newmont (NYSE:NEM) set to snap six consecutive sessions of gains, as the company’s shares are trading 2.78% lower at $90.48 on Thursday.

In the preceding six sessions, the company gained over 17.96%, compared to a 1.17% rise in the S&P 500 Index. However, shares of the company have gone down by over 147.07% compared to a gain of 16.48% in the broader S&P 500 Index in 2025.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, NEM has a strong BUY rating with a score of 4.95 out of 5. The company was rated A+ for profitability, while it got an A- for growth and an A- for momentum.

Seeking Alpha analyst Taylor Dart rated NEM a HOLD, reporting that although Newmont has deleveraged at breakneck speed in 2025, it won't have the same tailwind of considerable asset sales and will see lower output at higher costs in 2026. Also, NEM printed technical sell signals earlier this month above $90.00/share; he sees a risk of a deeper correction.

However, for Wall Street analysts, 17 rated the stock a Buy or above, 3 rated it a Hold, while 1 considered it a Sell and lower.

Similarly, Seeking Alpha analysts appeared rather bullish about the stock, issuing a Buy call for NEM.

Seeking Alpha analyst James Foord rated NEM a strong Buy, by reporting that Q3 results showed record cash flow, strong cost discipline, and a near debt-free status, with continued dividends and operational optimization.

In Q3 2025, the company received net cash proceeds of nearly $640M from asset and equity sales and generated a record cash flow of $640M.

He also added that stock trades below sector median valuations and discounted cash flow models suggest significant upside, supported by a bullish long-term gold outlook.

data.title: LKQ plans to sell specialty parts division - report
data.description:
LKQ Corp. (LKQ) is planning to sell its specialty parts business, Keystone Automotive Industries. 

The company has hired bankers at Bank of America to find a buyer for the unit, according to a Reuters report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The division may be worth about $1 billion. 

LKQ has been approached in the past by financial sponsors and began taking meetings with interested parties at a recent industry conference in Las Vegas, according to the report.LKQ declined to comment to Reuters. Shares of LKQ rose 2.3% and have dropped 15% this year.

data.title: Tesla leads down day for the electric vehicle sector as headwinds mount
data.description:
The electric vehicle sector underperformed on Thursday amid persistent concerns about global electric vehicle demand, the competitive pricing backdrop in China, and general risk-off trading across the market. There have also been more indications that electric vehicle demand in the U.S. was pulled forward to August and September due to the expiration of the EV tax credit to a larger degree than originally forecast.

Aptera (SEV) was the biggest decliner, with a 12.2% drop, while Polestar Automotive (PSNY) shed 7.7%. Tesla (TSLA) was one of the leading decliners in the S&P 500 Index, with a 7.1% swing lower. Lucid Group (LCID) -7.2%, Rivian Automotive (RIVN) -6.6%, NIO (NIO) -4.0%, and VinFast Auto (VFS) -3.4% were also down more than the broad market.

The EV sector also woke up to the news that Waymo (WAYMO) has started offering fully autonomous freeway rides across the San Francisco Bay, Phoenix, and Los Angeles regions. "We're offering freeway access to a growing number of public riders and will introduce the service to more over time, including as we expand freeway capabilities to Austin, Atlanta, and beyond," Waymo said.

Analysts have highlighted that a rapid expansion by Waymo (WAYMO) and Uber (UBER) of their autonomous fleets could pose another headwind for Tesla (TSLA) as it looks to build a large-scale robotaxi network.

data.title: Kura Oncology wins FDA approval for leukemia drug
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on Thursday approved ziftomenib (Komzifti), a leukemia drug developed by Kura Oncology (KURA) with Japanese drugmaker Kyowa Kirin (OTCPK:KYKOF) (OTCPK:KYKOY), triggering a trading halt in the California-based biotech.

In a press release, the FDA said it has approved the menin inhibitor for adults with relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia ((AML)) with a nucleophosmin 1 (NPM1) genetic mutation for whom satisfactory alternative treatments are not available.

Kura (KURA) shares added ~2% when the stock resumed trading after the companies announced the approval, which is backed by data from their pivotal KOMET-001 trial. The first-in-human open-label study showed 21.4% of complete remission and CR with partial hematologic recovery following the once-daily therapy.

Previously, Kura’s (KURA) new drug application for ziftomenib was under the FDA’s priority review with a target action date of November 30.

“Bolstered by a strong balance sheet and our productive partnership with Kyowa Kirin, we are well-positioned to advance ziftomenib toward commercialization,” CEO Troy Wilson said with the company’s Q3 results last week.

Update 1.19 PM ET: Adds latest stock move

data.title: Disney targets double-digit EPS growth and $7B share repurchases in 2026 as direct-to-consumer and Experiences segments accelerate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Walt Disney Company (DIS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Robert Iger stated, "This was another year of great progress as we strengthened the company by leveraging the value of our creative and brand assets and continue to make meaningful progress in our direct-to-consumer businesses, resulting in strong earnings growth for the company." Iger highlighted that adjusted EPS for fiscal 2025 was up 19% from fiscal 2024, and the company delivered a 19% compound annual growth rate in adjusted EPS over the past three fiscal years.
  • For fiscal 2026, Iger announced, "We expect to deliver double-digit adjusted EPS growth compared to the prior year. The expected growth in earnings and cash flow enable us to continue investing in our businesses and to increase our return of capital to shareholders. We are targeting $7 billion in share repurchases in 2026, double the $3.5 billion we repurchased in fiscal 2025. We're also pleased to announce that the Board has declared a cash dividend of $1.50 per share, a 50% increase over the $1 paid to shareholders in fiscal 2025."
  • Iger emphasized the performance of Disney's film studios, noting, "Disney's live-action Lilo & Stitch remains the highest grossing Hollywood film at the global box office this calendar year. The film achieved 14.3 million views during its first 5 days on Disney+, becoming the second biggest Disney live-action premiere on the platform ever. Retail sales for Stitch from our Consumer Products business also continues to grow, eclipsing $4 billion in fiscal 2025."
  • Iger pointed out, "Over the past 2 years, our studios have delivered 4 global franchise hits that have earned more than $1 billion each, while no other Hollywood studio has achieved a single one during the same period."
  • Iger also highlighted, "Our streaming business had another quarter of profit growth with operating income up 39% in Q4. For the full year, we hit $1.3 billion in operating income, up $1.2 billion from last year and $300 million ahead of our original guidance."
  • CFO Hugh Johnston said, "Because of the timing on tax payments, the reported number is closer to 7%, driven by a couple of things. #1, obviously, OI growth is quite strong. #2, we've been investing for a couple of years and we've now sort of leveled off in terms of those levels of investment. That's something that we think you can look forward to in the out years, continued strong free cash flow growth from Disney, which obviously gives us a lot of flexibility in terms of the ability to return cash to shareholders, which was evidenced today by the doubling in the share repurchase and the 50% increase in the dividend. So we feel very good about the free cash flow growth going forward."

Outlook

  • Management projects double-digit adjusted EPS growth for fiscal 2026 compared to the prior year and targets $7 billion in share repurchases in 2026.
  • The Board declared a cash dividend of $1.50 per share, a 50% increase over the $1 paid in fiscal 2025.
  • No explicit changes to guidance from the previous quarter were provided, but management reiterated confidence in ongoing growth and capital returns.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EPS for fiscal 2025 was up 19% from fiscal 2024.
  • For the full year, direct-to-consumer operating income was $1.3 billion, up $1.2 billion from last year and $300 million ahead of original guidance.
  • Retail sales for Stitch from the Consumer Products business exceeded $4 billion in fiscal 2025.
  • Experiences segment delivered record operating income for both Q4 and the full year, with Q4 up 13% and the full year up 8% compared to the prior year.

Q&A

  • Benjamin Swinburne, Morgan Stanley: Asked about ESPN's direct-to-consumer product adoption and engagement. Iger responded that the ESPN launch "has been a real success for a number of reasons," attracting new users and high engagement with new features, noting "about 80% have signed up to what we call the trio bundle, which includes Disney+ and Hulu."
  • Steven Cahall, Wells Fargo: Asked about the studio slate and growth expectations. Iger stated, "We are very bullish on the slate ahead... including the Mandalorian, Toy Story 5, live-action Moana and then we're going to finish the calendar year with Avengers: Doomsday."
  • Robert Fishman, MoffettNathanson: Asked about Disney+ evolving into a super app and DTC revenue growth. Iger described the rollout of significant technology and personalization changes, while Johnston affirmed aspirations for double-digit top-line growth in DTC.
  • Jessica Reif Cohen, BofA Securities: Inquired about M&A and advertising. Johnston noted no plans for major M&A, expressed satisfaction with the current IP portfolio, and described improving advertising trends and expectations for advertising growth in 2026.
  • Michael Morris, Guggenheim: Probed Experiences segment drivers and NBA rights. Johnston highlighted cruise as a key growth driver and stated bookings are up 3% for the first quarter and for the year.
  • John Hodulik, UBS: Asked about domestic parks and cruise margins. Johnston said demand was in line with expectations, cruise utilization strong, and margins attractive but not disclosed.
  • Kutgun Maral, Evercore ISI: Inquired about DTC cost structure and 53rd week impact. Johnston reiterated focus on revenue growth with some SG&A savings and deferred specifics on the 53rd week until Q4.
  • David Karnovsky, JPMorgan: Asked about generative AI. Iger detailed productive conversations with AI companies for both protection of IP and creating engagement, and saw efficiency opportunities across Disney.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed curiosity and cautious optimism, probing for clarity around adoption rates, cost structure, M&A intentions, and growth sustainability. Tone was neutral to slightly positive, with several questions on competitive positioning and technology investments.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during both prepared remarks and Q&A, highlighting achievements and reiterating guidance. Phrases such as "we feel very good about the free cash flow growth going forward" and "we are very bullish on the slate ahead" signaled strong confidence. The defensiveness was minimal, with most answers direct and detailed.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, there was a slight increase in confidence from management, particularly in capital return strategies and DTC profitability, while analysts maintained a similar level of constructive inquiry.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter emphasized a substantial increase in capital returns, including a targeted $7 billion in share repurchases and a 50% increase in dividends for 2026, which were not highlighted in the previous quarter.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward direct-to-consumer profitability, technological advancements (including AI), and expansion in Experiences, especially cruises.
  • Analysts maintained focus on DTC growth, content slate strength, and Experiences segment performance, with additional questions on bundling and AI this quarter.
  • Management's tone grew more assertive in outlining growth plans and capital allocation, contrasting with the prior quarter’s emphasis on integration and strategic partnerships.

Risks and Concerns

  • The ongoing carriage dispute with YouTube TV was mentioned, with Johnston stating, "we built a hedge into that with the expectation that these discussions could go for a little while." Iger added, "our priority has always been to remain on their service without interruption... The deal that we have proposed is equal to or better than what other large distributors have already agreed to."
  • Management noted the importance of protecting IP in the context of generative AI and ongoing negotiations with AI companies.
  • No major concerns were raised about demand in domestic parks or cruise, with demand characterized as strong and in line with expectations.

Final Takeaway

Disney closed fiscal 2025 with strong growth in adjusted EPS, profitability in direct-to-consumer, and record performance in Experiences, while setting ambitious targets for capital return in 2026. Management's confidence was underscored by the success of new content, technological innovation, and disciplined expansion, with particular focus on leveraging its IP portfolio and deepening consumer engagement across platforms. The company’s emphasis on double-digit EPS growth and robust share repurchases signals a forward-looking approach to shareholder value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
